violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Owner/operator is currently taking an approved course in food safety management. Once successfully completed, she is to register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

Ambient temperature of the grab n go unit was observed held at 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Sandwiches were relocated to a properly functioning refrigeration unit. Front curtain was closed over the unit and ambient temperature decreased to 40 degrees F during inspection. Curtain is to remain closed if sandwiches or other TCS foods are to be stored in this unit. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025
Facility is lacking an allergen statement. An allergen statement must be posted in view of the customer (on display case, at the register...). New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. An employee certified in food safety management must register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. Application provided. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. PIC set up sanitizing bay of the 3-bay sink during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Soup, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Soup observed in a large container with a temperature of 43 degrees F. Soup was divided into smaller containers during inspection. Proper cooling methods reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

Thermometer is lacking in the walk-in cooler. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 06/19/2025

The doors to the outside, located at the entrance, are not tight-fitting. Space observed between the doors. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025
Bags of bread and cabbage were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (on top of plumbing pipe beside the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 06/23/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine, walk-in condenser fan guards, and shelving above the 3-bay sink have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2025
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine is being drained to be properly cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024

Ambient temperature of grab n go unit observed at 44 degrees F. Ham sandwich observed at 44 degrees F as well. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. TCS foods had been placed in this unit less than 2 hours prior to inspection and were moved to a properly functioning unit during inspection. Grab n go unit is to be serviced and is not to be used until it is maintaining a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024

Sliced deli meats and soups observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024
